title: Decide RepoBrief public distribution license
goal: Make an explicit owner-approved decision whether RepoBrief receives a public distribution license, review third-party obligations, and replace the restrictive project LicenseRef only with documented authorization.

acceptance.assertion: The copyright holder records an explicit keep-restricted or public-license decision; repository visibility is not treated as permission.
acceptance.verifier: Owner decision record and reviewed license diff or explicit restrictive closeout

acceptance.assertion: Third-party dependency and bundled-content license obligations relevant to distribution are reviewed and recorded.
acceptance.verifier: Dependency/content license inventory and review receipt

acceptance.assertion: Release and CI publication remain fail-closed until the decision and required notices are merged and verified.
acceptance.verifier: Release contract, workflow tests and GitHub release state

acceptance.assertion: The task does not claim legal advice, universal jurisdictional sufficiency, product readiness or publication authorization beyond the recorded scope.

validation: Lenskit PR #988 was merged at e241b7b95f08905159cf94f706f3e45024da4b71 from reviewed head 83f022515c4c88dfaf9e154bf7be6eaf17dd9adb.
All seven GitHub workflow runs observed for the exact PR head completed successfully: task-index, lint, ai-context guard, Doc-Freshness, contracts-validate, CodeQL and test-suite.
A fresh isolated readback of Lenskit main at 40b5f02c31cf213074a30529124d2ca67fc8f096 passed scripts/release/check_identity_distribution_decisions.py.
The focused current-main decision suite passed: 4 tests passed.
The current-main release contract passed with no findings and retains public_distribution_permission among its explicit non-claims.
Current workflow, release-script and release-documentation scanning found no GitHub artifact upload or release-publication action.
GitHub release readback returned an empty list; no RepoBrief/Lenskit GitHub Release is published.
Current main still contains the All-Rights-Reserved LICENSE, restrictive licensing boundary, explicit machine-readable decision, bounded third-party review and non-public release-candidate policy.
metadata.outcome: The copyright holder decision is complete and merged: RepoBrief remains publicly readable but no public redistribution license is granted. The exact third-party review is recorded, public release remains fail-closed, and the decision explicitly avoids legal, jurisdictional, readiness or redistribution claims. No additional Lenskit change was necessary.

verified_acceptance.rbv1-t020-owner-decision: The merged owner decision explicitly selects do_not_grant_public_redistribution_license and preserves LicenseRef-RepoBrief-All-Rights-Reserved; repository visibility is not treated as permission.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t020-obligations: The exact locked Python environment was inventoried: 57 packages, 53 identified, 4 metadata-ambiguous and 0 unresolved; the source candidate vendors none of them and the report explicitly does not grant redistribution clearance.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t020-publication-boundary: The release policy and current contract allow deterministic internal candidates only, publish no artifacts, expose no GitHub Releases, and remain blocked_without_separate_written_permission.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t020-non-claims: The decision explicitly does not establish legal advice, universal enforceability, redistribution permission, permanent rejection of open source or product readiness.
